2606.17798.title: LiveStarPro: Proactive Streaming Video Understanding with Hierarchical Memory for Long-Horizon Streams
2606.17798.paper_summary: Despite the remarkable progress of Video Large Language Models (Video-LLMs), current online architectures still struggle to simultaneously process continuous video streams, decide autonomously when to respond, and preserve long-horizon contextual memory. These obstacles undermine real-time responsiveness and cause severe forgetting throughout prolonged interactions. In this work, we introduce LiveStarPro, a live streaming assistant that is designed for proactive video understanding over long-horizon streams. The design of LiveStarPro rests on three complementary components. The first component is Streaming Verification Decoding (SVeD), an inference framework that identifies the appropriate response timing through single-pass perplexity verification, thereby eliminating the dependency on explicit silence tokens. The second component is Streaming Causal Attention Masks (SCAM), a training strategy that enforces incremental video-language alignment over variable-length streams. The third component is Tree-Structured Hierarchical Memory (TSHM), a recursive memory architecture that organizes evicted historical information into event chains and consequently enables efficient retrieval from effectively unbounded video streams. To facilitate a comprehensive evaluation under realistic online conditions, we further present OmniStarPro, a large-scale benchmark that spans 15 diverse real-world scenarios and that extends to hour-scale streams for the assessment of long-term recall. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LiveStarPro consistently surpasses existing methods, attaining a 28.9% improvement in semantic correctness and an 18.2% reduction in timing error, while its streaming key-value cache further yields a 1.58x inference speedup over the same model without caching. The model and the code are publicly available at https://github.com/sotayang/LiveStarPro.
2606.17798.paper_summary_zh: 儘管視頻大型語言模型（Video-LLMs）取得了顯著進展，但當前的在線架構仍然難以同時處理連續視頻流、自主決定何時回應，以及保留長期的上下文記憶。這些障礙削弱了實時響應能力，並在長時間互動中造成嚴重的遺忘。在這項工作中，我們介紹了LiveStarPro，一個旨在對長期視頻流進行主動理解的直播助手。LiveStarPro的設計基於三個互補的組件。第一個組件是流式驗證解碼（SVeD），這是一個推理框架，通過單次通過的困惑度驗證來識別適當的回應時機，從而消除了對明確靜音標記的依賴。第二個組件是流式因果注意力掩碼（SCAM），這是一種訓練策略，強制在可變長度的流上進行增量視頻-語言對齊。第三個組件是樹狀層次記憶（TSHM），這是一種遞歸記憶架構，將驅逐的歷史信息組織成事件鏈，從而實現從實際無界限的視頻流中高效檢索。為了在現實的在線條件下促進全面評估，我們進一步提出了OmniStarPro，一個涵蓋15個多樣化真實世界場景的大規模基準，並擴展到小時級別的流，以評估長期回憶。大量實驗表明，LiveStarPro始終超越現有方法，在語義正確性上提高了28.9%，在時間誤差上減少了18.2%，同時其流式鍵值緩存進一步實現了相較於不使用緩存的同一模型1.58倍的推理加速。該模型和代碼可在https://github.com/sotayang/LiveStarPro公開獲取。
2606.17798.author: Zhenyu Yang et.al.
2606.17798.authors: Zhenyu Yang, Kairui Zhang, Bing Wang, Shengsheng Qian, Changsheng Xu
